events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region
events.description: The war in the Middle East continues, with attacks causing further terror and suffering, deepening the humanitarian crisis across the region. The UN announced the launch of a task force to meet the immense needs. In Geneva, diplomats at the Human Rights Council have been discussing the school strike in Iran’s Minab that killed more than 100 children. Stay with us for live updates on this and from UN agencies providing relief. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region

events.title: Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake
events.description: A “new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment,” a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthquake.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake

events.title: Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears
events.description: Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with “no safe space” to go.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Vital food aid blocked, Security Council meets in emergency session on Lebanon
events.description: More than a month since war erupted in the Middle East, UN agencies confirmed on Tuesday that huge numbers of people have returned to Syria from Lebanon "exhausted, traumatized and with very, very few belongings". Meanwhile, the UN International Maritime Organization said that another vessel has been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, increasing concerns of further delays in transporting lifesaving aid. The Security Council met in New York in emergency session on Lebanon. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Vital food aid blocked, Security Council meets in emergency session on Lebanon

events.title: At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack
events.description: Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s breadbasket Artibonite region, significantly more than official estimates, a human rights group has said. Police initially reported 16 dead and 10 injured, while a preliminary report from civil protection authorities suggested 17 had died and 19 were wounded. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack

events.title: Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas
events.description: Football federation president on the run with wife and son Conviction in absentia of wide-ranging corruption charges Authorities in Congo-Brazzaville have applied to Interpol for an international arrest warrant against Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas, the president of the country’s football federation, Fecofoot, after he was convicted of embezzling $1.1m in Fifa funds. Mayolas is on the run with his wife and son after they were all sentenced to life imprisonment this month for embezzling funds provided by world football’s governing body as part of its Covid-19 relief plan in February 2021. As the Guardian revealed last year, that included almost $500,000 earmarked for the Congo women’s team. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Interpol arrest warrant requested in Congo-Brazzaville for Jean-Guy Blaise Mayolas

events.title: World News in Brief: Ukraine drone attacks, Afghan rights, Zero-Waste Day
events.description: A night of drone attacks reportedly killed two people and injured 12 in the Ukrainian port city of Odesa, as a maternity hospital and three educational facilities were also damaged.

events.title: Peacekeepers killed by roadside explosion in Lebanon, initial report finds
events.description: The deaths of the two Indonesian nationals came less than a day after another UN peacekeeper - also Indonesian - was killed in Lebanon.

events.title: As Ukraine commemorates the Bucha massacre, Russia rejects idea of Easter truce
events.description: Ukraine is commemorating the fourth anniversary of the Bucha massacre, named after a town who many of its residents are thought to have been massacred by Russian invading troops in the early weeks of the war. Since, the conflict hasn't slowed down, as Russia keeps pounding the country. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As Ukraine commemorates the Bucha massacre, Russia rejects idea of Easter truce

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes
events.description: Further attacks have been reported across the Middle East as the war enters a second month, with two more Indonesian peacekeepers killed in Southern Lebanon on Monday, following the death of a fellow ‘blue helmet’ a day earlier. On the diplomatic front, the UN has announced a taskforce to restore the flow of fertilizer and aid through the Strait of Hormuz, while the UN's atomic watchdog confirms an attack on a heavy water facility at Khondab in Iran. Stay with us for live updates on this and UN agencies. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 30 March: UN peacekeepers killed amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes

events.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
events.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world

events.title: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
events.description: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?

events.title: #Iran: Massive explosion in Isfahan
events.description: Images posted on social media on March 31 have captured a huge #explosion in the Iranian city of #Isfahan. A local security official said strikes had targeted military locations in the city, according to Iran’s Fars agency. Isfahan is home to a #nuclear enrichment site.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 #Iran: Massive explosion in Isfahan

events.title: Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida
events.description: More than 1,700 people were killed and nearly 200,000 more displaced in the July 2025 massacres in Sweida, Syria, UN human rights investigators said on Friday in a report that called for greater action towards accountability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida

events.title: Ukraine: Top EU diplomats mark 4 years since Bucha massacre
events.description: Brussels' chief diplomat and several EU foreign ministers are in Ukraine to mark four years since one of Russia's most infamous atrocities. The Bucha massacre, just weeks into the full-scale war, shocked the world.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Top EU diplomats mark 4 years since Bucha massacre

events.title: How countries are dealing with rising oil and gas prices
events.description: With the US-Iran conflict continuing, governments worldwide are desperately trying to curb the effect of soaring energy prices on populations.

events.title: Lebanon at ‘breaking point’ as displacement soars and strikes intensify
events.description: The UN’s top humanitarian official warned the Security Council on Tuesday that Lebanon is facing one of its most dangerous moments in years, with escalating violence, mass displacement and deepening human suffering pushing the country to “breaking point”.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon at ‘breaking point’ as displacement soars and strikes intensify

events.title: How Pakistan positioned itself at the center of global crisis management
events.description: Pakistan has emerged as a global crisis mediator amid the US war on Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Pakistan positioned itself at the center of global crisis management

events.title: Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East
events.description: Foreign ministers Ishaq Dar and Wang Yi met in Beijing as Pakistan pushes for peacemaker role Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan and China have released a joint five-part proposal for peace in the Middle East, after Pakistan’s foreign minister flew to Beijing on Tuesday to seek Chinese support for the country’s faltering efforts to negotiate an end to end the war. The one-day meeting between Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, came as Pakistan continues to push for the role of peacemaker between the United States and Iran, even as the war shows little sign of relenting. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East

events.title: Australia politics live: ministers wary of Trump’s ‘get your own oil’ comment; health insurance premiums rise today
events.description: Government in February approved an average premium increase of 4.41% from today. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Butler tells states to go back to the table on GST windfall return After failing to reach an agreement yesterday, Mark Butler hopes the states will come back to the table and to discuss returning some of the GST windfall from higher fuel prices. They did agree at the National Cabinet on Monday to pass this windfall gain back to motorists. We wanted that to happen through an arrangement between the Commonwealth and the states whereby we’d be able to deliver an additional cut to the fuel excise, which would flow in the same way through to bowsers. But they haven’t yet been able to agree that amongst themselves. We want them to do that. We want them to get back around the table over the course of the next day or so. I think in the scale of late night missives from Donald Trump, this is nothing particularly out of the ordinary. When it comes to the conflict in Iran, we have a pretty simple view … we want to see de-escalation of this conflict, not escalation. And we want to see a clear timeframe for how this conflict will be brought to an end. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia politics live: ministers wary of Trump’s ‘get your own oil’ comment; health insurance premiums rise today

events.title: Iran's allies could close second crucial sea route, with 'clear and significant' impact on UK
events.description: The entry of Yemen's Houthi rebels into the war on the side of Iran has stoked fears that the oil crisis affecting global trade could get worse.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's allies could close second crucial sea route, with 'clear and significant' impact on UK

events.title: Oil-thirsty Asian nations seek Russian crude as Iran war strains supplies
events.description: Asian nations are increasingly competing for Russian crude oil as an energy crisis mounts amid the U.S. and Israeli war in Iran, which has choked off roughly a fifth of the world’s oil supply
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil-thirsty Asian nations seek Russian crude as Iran war strains supplies

events.title: International break: Germany's World Cup winners and losers
events.description: In the international friendlies before the FIFA World Cup, some players on Germany's national football team made the most of their chances, while others missed the opportunity to impress.

events.title: US trade chief lambasts WTO after failed talks
events.description: The US has slammed the World Trade Organization's failure to reach consensus on a key e-commerce moratorium. The top US trade official said he sees only a limited role for the international body.

events.title: Can Lebanon keep it together? Government faces Israeli invasion, standoff with Hezbollah
events.description: The Lebanese have seen it all, from civil war to the 2019 protests demanding an end to revolving door politics, from the 2020 port of Beirut explosion that exposed the tragic consequences of nepotism to Israel’s 2024 operation to neutralize longtime foe Hezbollah. Now Israel’s ground forces are back to again take on Tehran-backed foe Hezbollah. Thus, Lebanon finds itself the second front of the month-long war launched against Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Can Lebanon keep it together? Government faces Israeli invasion, standoff with Hezbollah

events.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
events.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say

events.title: At least 16 killed and thousands displaced by gang attack in rural Haiti
events.description: The attack happened in the Artibonite region, where members of the Gran Grif gang have targeted locals in the past.

events.title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad by suspected Iranian-backed armed group
events.description: A woman US journalist who was kidnapped in Iraq's capital city by a suspected Iranian-backed Iraqi armed group, US officials said on Tuesday. Advocacy groups identified the missing woman as Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist based in Rome who has covered several wars in the Middle East..
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad by suspected Iranian-backed armed group

events.title: Report finds there are no safe places for women in Darfur
events.description: In tonight's edition, Doctors Without Borders sounds the alarm over Sudan, warning sexual violence against women and girls is being used as a weapon of war. Also, a Tunisian ⁠court handed down a ​two-year prison sentence to news website editor Ghassen Ben Khelifa in ​the latest prosecution targeting media workers. And after stops in New York, London and Melbourne, the Africa Fashion exhibition arrives in Paris.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Report finds there are no safe places for women in Darfur

events.title: Gulf allies privately make the case to Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
events.description: Gulf states, led by Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, are urging Trump to continue prosecuting the war against Iran, arguing that Tehran hasn't been weakened enough by the bombing campaign, according to U.S., Gulf and Israeli officials. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Dr H. A. Hellyer, Senior Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute. He stresses that, despite some Gulf States pressing to prolong the war, none of the Gulf States want the Iranian state to collapse.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf allies privately make the case to Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated

events.title: Activists in Haiti say at least 30 people are dead after a gang attacks a town again
events.description: Human rights activists in Haiti say at least 30 people are dead and dozens more are missing after a gang renewed its attack on a town in the country's central region

events.title: Teen cyclist killed at Road World Championships not found for 82 minutes
events.description: A cyclist who was killed during the Road World Championships was not found for 82 minutes after a crash, investigators find.

events.title: Russia allegedly sharing satellite intelligence on US bases with Iran, world leader claims
events.description: Zelenskyy warns Russian satellites photographed U.S. military bases across the Middle East in Iran's interests, signaling possible strike preparations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia allegedly sharing satellite intelligence on US bases with Iran, world leader claims

events.title: Hiring in the US drops to pandemic lows as job market under Trump stagnates
events.description: Labour market stagnation mirrors a slowing private payroll growth, averaging just 18,000 monthly in recent months

events.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
events.description: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

events.title: Lebanon condemns Israel’s 'clear intention' to impose new occupation of its territory
events.description: Lebanon said Israeli strikes hit near Beirut’s airport road on Tuesday as the UN Security Council met after three peacekeepers were killed. Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said troops would occupy parts of southern Lebanon post-war, prompting Beirut to warn of a “clear intention” to impose a new occupation.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Lebanon condemns Israel’s 'clear intention' to impose new occupation of its territory

events.title: US fuel prices surge past $4 a gallon
events.description: The average price of regular gasoline in the United States topped $4 dollars a gallon for the first time since 2022, as the Iran war continues to disrupt global energy supply. This as inflation in the Eurozone rose past the ECB's target to 2.5% in March. Meanwhile, Egyptian shops and restaurants are having to close early due to the government's energy-saving measures.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US fuel prices surge past $4 a gallon

events.title: Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan
events.description: Fears are mounting for civilians caught up in Sudan’s deadly war between rival militaries as attacks intensify and humanitarian access shrinks, following a deadly airstrike on a funeral gathering in West Kordofan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan

events.title: Capital gains discount and negative gearing turbocharged property speculation and pushed up prices, analysis finds
events.description: Exclusive: Landlords ‘leveraging up’ by exploiting property tax rules are fuelling Australia’s housing affordability crisis, e61 Institute finds Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The combination of the capital gains tax discount and negative gearing rules has turbocharged debt-fuelled property speculation over recent decades, according to a new analysis of hundreds of thousands of property investments. The federal budget in three weeks’ time is widely expected to include changes to tax breaks for investors , in an effort to rebalance the tax system away from the wealthiest Australians and to take pressure off home prices . Continue reading...

events.title: Zimbabwe split as public weighs electoral reforms bill
events.description: Zimbabwe has begun nationwide public hearings on a proposed bill that could extend President Mnangagwa's term and overhaul the electoral system. Supporters call it stabilizing, but critics warn it threatens democracy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zimbabwe split as public weighs electoral reforms bill

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.description: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for months. When the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?

events.title: Israel to occupy swaths of south Lebanon and destroy houses near border
events.description: Israel's defence minister has vowed his army will block residents from returning to southern Lebanon after the war and will be destroying all homes in villages along the border, in which he implied would be a Gaza-like scale of destruction as seen in Rafah and Beit Hanoun. More than 1200 people have been killed in Lebanon and over 1.2 million displaced since Israel began its offensive against Hezbollah at the start of March.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel to occupy swaths of south Lebanon and destroy houses near border

events.title: Syria: Hundreds of thousands flee Lebanon, vital food aid blocked
events.description: The trauma of mass displacement and disruption to aid shipments throughout the world are among the devastating impacts of the war raging in the Middle East, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Syria: Hundreds of thousands flee Lebanon, vital food aid blocked

events.title: ‘No precedent’ for seafarers caught in war zone in post-WW2 era
events.description: Some 20,000 seafarers remain stranded on ships in the Strait of Hormuz as the war in the Middle East continues, a situation which has been described as unprecedented in the post-Second World War era.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘No precedent’ for seafarers caught in war zone in post-WW2 era

events.title: Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments
events.description: Just hours after war broke out in the Middle East last month, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned the Security Council that the fighting risked “igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region of the world.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments

events.title: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
events.description: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

events.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
events.description: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war

events.title: Israeli air attack kills father and two-year-old son in Gaza
events.description: A father and his two-year-old son were amongst six people killed by Israeli forces in Gaza on Tuesday.

events.title: Ukraine to ask US mediators to pass on Easter ceasefire offer to Russia
events.description: Ukraine's president hosts EU leaders to mark Bucha massacre anniversary, says negotiator Umerov in Turkiye for talks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine to ask US mediators to pass on Easter ceasefire offer to Russia

events.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
events.description: As the war in Iran enters its second month, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranians debate whether the war is worth it

events.title: A U.S. journalist is kidnapped in Iraq after threats from Iran-backed groups
events.description: The Al-Monitor news site identified the kidnapped journalist as Shelly Kittleson. The State Department says she had been warned of threats and it's working with the FBI to get her freed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A U.S. journalist is kidnapped in Iraq after threats from Iran-backed groups

events.title: Has Pakistan outplayed India in Iran war diplomacy?
events.description: Pakistan is gaining diplomatic visibility during the Iran conflict, while questions are growing over India's quieter strategy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Has Pakistan outplayed India in Iran war diplomacy?

events.title: EU faces 'largest oil supply disruption in history', energy expert says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Francesco Sassi, Assistant Professor at the University of Oslo and energy geopolitics expert, says that the Iran war has provoked "the largest oil supply disruption in history" adding that "what is really concerning is that national governments will start to unilaterally take action, subsidising energy consumption or act to secure energy supplies, and this will inevitably increase the cost of natural gas and oil in Europe".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU faces 'largest oil supply disruption in history', energy expert says

events.title: Australian police fatally shoot suspect in 3-hour standoff after 2 officers killed
events.description: Australian police believe they have shot dead a suspect accused of killing two police officers and seriously wounding a third in a remote forest region seven months ago

events.title: UN peacekeeper killed, another seriously injured, in southern Lebanon
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for accountability after a peacekeeper was killed in Lebanon on Sunday amid hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Michigan synagogue attacker was inspired by Hezbollah, sought to kill as many Jewish people as possible: feds
events.description: Federal investigators say the Michigan synagogue attacker was radicalized by Hezbollah and reportedly sought to kill as many Jewish people as possible on March 12.

events.title: Iran's 'basement' Chinese drone networks spark fears of sleeper cell attacks on US soil
events.description: Draganfly's Cameron Chell warns Iran could produce over 100,000 FPV drones monthly, with swarm attacks on U.S. soil possible within eight months.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's 'basement' Chinese drone networks spark fears of sleeper cell attacks on US soil

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Sad faces all round as Bolivia’s clowns protest over decree threatening their livelihoods
events.description: Clowns in Bolivia are upset by mandate that stops schools hosting events from which they earn a living Dozens of clowns have marched through the streets of Bolivia’s capital to protest against a government decree that limits extracurricular activities in schools, threatening their livelihoods. Wearing full face paint and their signature red noses, the clowns gathered on Monday in front of the ministry of education in La Paz to oppose a decree published in February. The new mandate says schools must comply with 200 days of lessons each year – in effect banning them from hosting the special events where the entertainers are frequently employed. Continue reading...

events.title: Labor’s draft party platform more assertive on China and omits mandatory jail term stance
events.description: Exclusive: the 2026 document is designed to provide ‘scaffolding’ for a long-term Labor government Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor’s longstanding opposition to mandatory jail terms has been omitted from the first draft of its new national party platform, after the Albanese government backed minimum sentences on several occasions. An early working draft of the ALP’s updated platform also includes more assertive language on China and seeks to position Australia as an “active middle power” in an increasingly fraught and contested world. Continue reading...

events.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
events.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.

events.title: Alleged Scottish crime boss set to be deported to Spain after arrest in Bali
events.description: A Scottish man who is allegedly a high-ranking member of an international crime gang is set to be deported to Spain after being arrested in Bali.

events.title: Chile's president vows tougher school security following recent stabbing and firearms incidents
events.description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast says his government will tighten school security after two recent incidents involving weapons and amid growing concern about school violence

events.title: North Korea tests solid-fuel missile engine as Kim boosts threat to US mainland
events.description: Kim Jong Un oversaw a new solid-fuel rocket engine test, state media reports, as North Korea pushes to upgrade its long-range strategic strike capabilities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea tests solid-fuel missile engine as Kim boosts threat to US mainland

events.title: US reopens embassy in Venezuela in significant thawing of relations
events.description: Resumption of diplomatic operations come three months after former president Maduro was abducted Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US government is resuming operations at its embassy in Venezuela, the state department announced on Monday, nearly three months since former president Nicolás Maduro was abducted from the country and locked up in the US. The resumption of US diplomatic operations in Venezuela marks a significant step in the US-Venezuela relationship, as the Trump administration begins to work closely with the government of Delcy Rodríguez , the acting president who replaced Maduro after his forcible ousting by US troops. Rodríguez was Maduro’s vice-president. Continue reading...

events.title: Vanuatu Indigenous leaders raise concerns over plans to build resort for cruise tourists
events.description: Exclusive: Environmental impact assessments are ‘incomplete’, say leaders, and private beach club could harm fragile ecosystems Indigenous community leaders in Vanuatu have raised concerns over plans by the cruise operator Royal Caribbean to build a private beach club on the island of Lelepa, arguing environmental impact assessments by the company are “incomplete” and “misleading”. The community leaders outlined the issues in a letter sent to Royal Caribbean on 26 February, which has been seen by the Guardian. The leaders also said the development could harm fragile ecosystems and a nearby Unesco world heritage site. Continue reading...

events.title: Germany: Wolf bites woman in Hamburg
events.description: It is the first recorded wolf attack on a human since wolves returned to Germany nearly 30 years ago. The wolf likely wandered into the city by accident.

events.title: Burundi's main city rocked by blasts after electrical fire at military store
events.description: Multiple blasts hit Burundi's main city of Bujumbura on Tuesday after an electrical fire broke out at a military arsenal in the east African nation. An army spokesperson said emergency services were intervening and urged the public to remain calm after the incident sparked fears of a coup against the government.

events.title: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 in South Sudan after a dispute at a gold mine
events.description: Gunmen have killed more than 70 people in South Sudan after a dispute at a gold mining site near the capital, Juba

events.title: Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage
events.description: Uganda's military chief Gen. Muhoozi Kainerugaba claims Uganda could enter the Iran-Israel war on Israel's side, posting a series of pro-Israel statements on X this week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ugandan military chief vows to back Israel against Iran in viral social media barrage

events.title: Palestinians in occupied West Bank protest Israel’s death penalty law
events.description: Families of Palestinian prisoners protested against a bill allowing the death penalty for Palestinians in the West Bank
events.ticker_label: 💥 Palestinians in occupied West Bank protest Israel’s death penalty law

events.title: ‘God squad’ waives endangered species law to allow US drilling in Gulf of Mexico
events.description: Critics say exemption for fossil fuels exploits White House’s ‘self-made gas crisis’, and could doom the rare Rice’s whale Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A US government panel on Tuesday exempted oil and gas drilling in the Gulf of Mexico from the Endangered Species Act (ESA), a move which critics say could doom a rare whale species and harm other marine life . The Endangered Species Committee – which had not convened in more than three decades – voted to approve the request for the ESA exemption at the request of the defense secretary, Pete Hegseth . Continue reading...

events.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
events.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel

events.title: India news: Tennis legend Leander Paes joins Modi's BJP
events.description: The move comes just days before elections in West Bengal. In other news, Iran has called a US strike on a Delhi-bound plane a 'war crime' and India is gearing up for the survey of the decade. Follow DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India news: Tennis legend Leander Paes joins Modi's BJP

events.title: Europe pushes back on US military operations as concerns over Iran war mount
events.description: France and Italy have joined Spain in pushing back on U.S.-Israeli military operations, sources said on Tuesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump singled out NATO allies in Europe as unhelpful in the month-long war, highlighting divisions. The decisions came against a backdrop of tensions between Washington and key partners over the war in Iran. ​Earlier this month, Trump called ‌longtime NATO allies "cowards" over their lack of support against Iran. FRANCE 24's Eliza Herbert reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe pushes back on US military operations as concerns over Iran war mount

events.title: EU shows support to Ukraine at Bucha commemoration
events.description: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. Russian military forces retreated from Bucha in 2022, hundreds of bodies were discovered, including women, children and elderly people, many of the corpses had been left lying in the streets. A delegation of EU foreign ministers arrived in Kyiv on March 31 to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

events.title: Wolf bites woman in a shopping area in Germany's 2nd-biggest city
events.description: Authorities say a wolf has bitten a woman in a Hamburg shopping area, in what experts call Germany’s first known wild wolf attack on a person since 1998

events.title: Video shows police drone track down madman after alleged rampage with stabbing, shooting, crash into store
events.description: Las Vegas police released drone video showing officers tracking a violent suspect after a domestic incident involving an alleged vehicle ramming and stabbing.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Video shows police drone track down madman after alleged rampage with stabbing, shooting, crash into store

events.title: Iran's internet blackout hiding strike damage and suppressing dissent, Israeli officials say

events.title: Avi Lewis, elected to lead Canada’s New Democratic party, promises ‘NDP comeback’
events.description: Ex-TV host pledged to centre party around equity, with higher wealth taxes, green energy and tuition-free education Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Canada’s embattled New Democratic party (NDP) has elected the former broadcaster and self-proclaimed socialist Avi Lewis as its new leader, as it looks to rebuild following a devastating federal election last year that saw it lose official party status. A record number of members voted in the three-day NDP leadership convention, giving Lewis a first-ballot win that underscored widespread support. Lewis pledged to convert the “tremendous momentum” of the convention into an “NDP comeback”. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘A place where music fills the air’: Bangkok to host Eurovision’s first Asia song contest
events.description: Spin-off launched with 10 nations, as original event remains mired in protests and boycotts over Israel’s involvement Eurovision is seeking to expand into the Asian market by hosting a version of its song contest in Bangkok this year, just as the original annual event is being buffeted by discord and boycotts on the eve of its 70th anniversary edition. The grand final of the inaugural Eurovision song contest Asia will take place in Thailand’s capital on Saturday 14 November, the Switzerland-based organisation announced on Tuesday. Broadcasters from 10 countries have confirmed their participation. Continue reading...

events.title: Police hunt armed suspect after housekeeper bound, attacked in luxury mansion heist: report

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March

events.title: 'A New Day Has Come': Veritable treasure hunt as Eiffel Tower fêtes return of Celine Dion
events.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Dr. Julie Escurignan, Associate Professor of Management & Communication at the Université Catholique de l'Ouest. Dr. Escurignan explores the cultural, economic, and deeply personal dynamics surrounding Celine Dion’s highly anticipated return to the global stage. This comeback is not merely a concert series, she explains, rather a carefully orchestrated cultural event espousing the intimate narrative of resilience: The Paris residency represents an innovative campaign set in an iconic urban setting and the enduring power of a unique artistic legacy.

events.title: Wannabe burglars storm Real Housewives star’s mansion – only to get spooked by alarm, crash getaway car: cops

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
events.description: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days. The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet stream, which helped a deep area of low pressure to develop north of Saudi Arabia. This, in turn, drew moist tropical air from the Indian Ocean and triggered intense storms. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia

events.title: Urgent action needed to prevent surge in digital violence in Africa, experts say
events.description: A huge rise in internet users under the age of 30 has fuelled an increase in online violence against women and girls with devastating real-life effects, activists say Activists and lawyers in Africa are calling for urgent action to protect women, girls and boys as digital violence surges across the continent. A massive rise in internet users, coupled with huge numbers of people aged under 30 , has fuelled an increase in gendered online violence across the continent, according to experts, by giving perpetrators new tools to control and silence women and girls, and influence boys. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia’s superb fairywren could be extinct within decades due to climate crisis, researchers say
events.description: Scientists tracked bird population in Canberra’s botanic gardens and found climate impacts starting to affect them Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A common and well-loved bird of bush and garden could go extinct within 30-40 years due to the weather impacts of climate change, researchers say. Data derived from nearly 30 years of weekly observations tracked the lives of superb fairy wrens in Canberra’s botanic gardens, noting the changing weather’s impacts on them. Continue reading...

Gao, 69, who was detained in 2024 during a visit to China from the US, faces a maximum three-year prison sentence, his wife, Zhao Yaliang, and Shane Yi, a researcher at the Chinese human rights defenders group, said. events.title: Goodbye Graaff-Reinet: South African town’s name change stirs racial tensions

Petitions have been signed, rival marches held and a formal letter of complaint sent to the sports, arts and culture minister, Gayton McKenzie, who approved the name change on 6 February. Continue reading...

events.title: China to ban storing remains of dead in ‘bone ash apartments’
events.description: Practice of using apartments to store relatives’ ashes has risen as rapid urbanisation and ageing population increases competition for cemetery plots China is introducing a law to stop people storing the ashes of their dead relatives in empty high-rise flats rather than paying steep costs for increasingly scarce cemetery plots. China’s new funeral management legislation will prohibit the use of “residential housing specifically for the purpose of storing cremated remains” and the burial of corpses or construction of tombs in “areas other than public cemeteries”. Continue reading...

events.title: Deepwater discoveries: scientists find more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea
events.description: Brittlestars, sea anemones and a catshark among new-to-science species collected during expedition off the Queensland coast Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Marine scientists have discovered more than 110 new fish and invertebrate species in the Coral Sea – a figure they believe could exceed 200 as more are identified. The species were found in waters between 200 metres and 3km deep in the Coral Sea marine park, Australia’s largest marine protected area, which spans nearly 1m sq km to the east of the Great Barrier Reef. Continue reading...

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: One of the first people known to change their gender was an ancient Egyptian pharaoh
events.description: NPR art director and illustrator Jackie Lay tells the story of Hatshepsut, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest pharaohs in Egypt's history — but whose legacy was erased for over 3,000 years.

events.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
events.description: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon

events.title: Senegal: President signs off on LGBTQ law doubling maximum jail term for same-sex relations
events.description: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye enacted the new law after it resoundingly cleared parliament earlier this month. It also penalizes those "promoting" or funding same-sex relations, as well as false allegations.

events.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
events.description: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime

events.title: No, Iranian hackers didn’t leak a video of the FBI's Kash Patel dancing
events.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been the subject of social media buzz this week, after his private emails were hacked by an Iranian-linked group. There's also a viral Bollywood dance video being falsely linked to the incident, despite no connection to Patel, as Vedika Bahl clears up in Truth or Fake.

events.title: Tiger Woods pleads not guilty to driving under influence - as details of arrest released
events.description: Tiger Woods has pleaded not guilty to driving under the influence.

events.title: Ban on living on the outer Chagos Islands is overturned
events.description: A court has overturned a ban on people living on the outer Chagos Islands.

events.title: Drug-smuggling tunnel with rail system and underground crane discovered
events.description: Spanish police have discovered a tunnel in ‌the North African exclave of Ceuta, to smuggle tons of hashish from Morocco into Spain.

events.title: Paintings 'worth millions' stolen in three-minute heist
events.description: Three famous paintings worth millions have been stolen from a museum in northern Italy.

events.title: Man extradited from Scotland and charged with 2006 murder of ex-Sinn Fein official
events.description: A man has appeared in a Dublin court after being charged with the murder of a former Sinn Fein official who was shot dead 20 years ago.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Man extradited from Scotland and charged with 2006 murder of ex-Sinn Fein official

events.title: Fugitive accused of killing two police officers 'shot dead'
events.description: A fugitive accused of killing two police officers is believed to have been shot dead, Australian police have said.

events.title: Mongolia's third prime minister in a year faces political infighting and external crises
events.description: Mongolia’s parliament has confirmed Uchral Nyam-Osor as prime minister to break a political deadlock and try to calm economic fears

events.title: Meta, TikTok and YouTube aren't fully complying with account ban, Australia says
events.description: Australia is considering bringing court action against Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube after alleging they are not doing enough to keep Australian children younger than 16 off their platforms

events.title: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 people in South Sudan over gold mining row, police say
events.description: Unknown gunmen kill more than 70 people in South Sudan over gold mining row, police say

events.title: Cuba to introduce two new banknotes honoring women to ease inflation as crises persist
events.description: Cubans accustomed to shopping with wads of bills stuffed into bags because of skyrocketing inflation will get some relief Wednesday when two new high-denomination banknotes go into circulation

events.title: Russia expels British diplomat over spying allegations
events.description: Russia has expelled a British diplomat it accuses of spying

events.title: Former Suriname President Chandrikapersad Santokhi, who investigated 1982 political killings, dies
events.description: Former Suriname President Chandrikapersad Santokhi has died

events.title: Lebanon: The UN peacekeepers helping communities who’ve stayed behind
events.description: The killing of three UN peacekeepers in south Lebanon in the past two days has highlighted the grave dangers for the 10,000 military personnel who make up the bulk of UNIFIL, the UN Interim Force in Lebanon – and for the communities caught up in intense clashes between the Israeli military and Hezbollah fighters.

events.title: The image from Gaza that still haunts me: Palestine relief agency chief
events.description: Asking the softly spoken, veteran humanitarian worker Philippe Lazzarini how he feels as he comes to the end of his second term as the head of the UN agency for Palestinians, UNRWA, is perhaps an unfair question.

events.title: Developing countries are being priced out, in struggle for affordable finance
events.description: Developing countries are being priced out of the affordable finance they desperately need for sustainable development, with sovereign credit ratings often overstating risk and overlooking long-term economic potential, the UN said on Monday.

events.title: Sea mines clearance: A new dimension of difficulty
events.description: Clearing mines laid at sea can be an “extremely challenging and very dangerous” undertaking according to a UN mines expert.

events.title: Kidnapped and tortured on an infamous migration route to Saudi Arabia
events.description: An Ethiopian man describes how he was tortured by human traffickers as he went in search of his nephew on a now infamous migration route from the Horn of Africa through Yemen to Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Wisconsin high school teacher charged with sexual assault of students, allegedly gave minors wine
events.description: A 22-year-old special ed teacher faces up to 40 years in prison after allegedly having sex with 16-year-old students and providing minors with alcohol.

events.title: Canadian murder suspect on most-wanted list arrested in Mississippi, was in US illegally, DHS says
events.description: Adrian Vincent Walker, one of Canada's top 25 most wanted fugitives on suspicion of a deadly 2024 Toronto murder., was arrested in Tupelo, Mississippi.

events.title: Experts debunk Tyler Robinson's ballistics claim: 'Unable to identify is not the same as ruled out'
events.description: Robinson allegedly confessed to Kirk's murder in texts, but his defense points to inconclusive ATF bullet testing as potential exculpatory evidence.

events.title: Outrage grows as ICE detainer ignored in connection to killing of 24-year-old mother and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Nancy Grace slams sheriff’s handling of Guthrie case: ‘The fish stinks at the head’
events.description: Nancy Grace says the Nancy Guthrie abduction brought back memories of her fiance's 1979 murder, recalling that victims are "never the same" after a loved one is taken.

events.title: Father loses legal fight to halt euthanasia of 25-year-old daughter in Spain
events.description: Noelia Castillo Ramos' case reached the European Court of Human Rights before 25-year-old died in Barcelona on March 26, 2025.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Father loses legal fight to halt euthanasia of 25-year-old daughter in Spain

events.title: North Korean laborers describe brutal forced labor in Russia: 'Working like a cow, earning nothing'
events.description: A North Korean forced labor survivor describes earning $800 a month but keeping only $10, with passports seized and no way to leave Russian work sites.
